nodes.norm_label: # note: the .scad's rim_lip subtraction is a geometric no-op (it lies inside

nodes.label: Tactus (Deaf-Accessible Guitar-Coaching Wearable)
nodes.rationale: From a webcam + mic, recover what/where/which-finger/how-clean and turn it into one precise correction you feel and see; render music as a direct measurable signal→vibration transform.

nodes.label: AI/Determinism Split
nodes.rationale: ML lives only where the problem is unsolved (occluded fretting hand + cause-blind buzz); everything downstream is a deterministic, pointable transform — that split is the rigor and rebuts 'is the AI just making it up?'.

nodes.label: Honesty Discipline
nodes.rationale: A new sensory channel for music, not 'Deaf people can hear'; pressure reported ordinal (never Newtons); placement is coarse fingertip-to-wire distance, not centimeters.

nodes.label: Locked Software Architecture (Browser/Python split)
nodes.rationale: Browser owns vision+UI, Python owns audio+model+12-ch haptic out; they sync over one localhost WebSocket with timestamped vision features.

nodes.label: Two Latency Tiers
nodes.rationale: Immediate haptic is audio-only ~40ms; vision-fused correction is slower (~100ms+); a 10ms vision-fused per-note haptic is physically impossible (camera+MediaPipe+WS ≈ 50–80ms).

nodes.label: 12-Channel Body Map (6 back strings + 6 torso fret-zones)
nodes.rationale: 6 SK473 stereo boards = 12 amp channels (the binding constraint); all 12 sites torso-mounted, kept ≥4–5cm apart; the 2 reserved zones dropped.
nodes.norm_label: 12-channel body map (6 back strings + 6 torso fret-zones)

nodes.label: Frets 1–6 Scope Lock (1:1 fret→zone)
nodes.rationale: Record/coach/render only what 6 fret-zone motors can show; 12-via-intensity multiplexing is a post-hackathon extension and this also scopes the AIML data.

nodes.label: KHD 3Ω/5W Driver (de-housed actuator)
nodes.rationale: The SK473's own drivers, factory-matched to the PAM8403 so 3Ω is safe at felt level; mass-y so likely punchier than the 40mm; the bought 40mm are spares.

nodes.label: Actuator Coupling (contact-button puck)
nodes.rationale: A bare cone radiates into air (heard, not felt); a contact button on the dust-cap with a rigid backer on the magnet couples cone excursion into tissue. Make-or-break risk.

nodes.label: Sequential Strum Sweep
nodes.rationale: Chords render as a staggered low→high sweep so ≤2 drivers fire at once → real peak ~2–3W (the all-on 33W case never happens) and a wrong note feels like a wrong spot in the sweep.

nodes.label: Markerless Fretboard Registration
nodes.rationale: Fit the fretboard homography from the guitar's own geometry (the instrument is its own ruler), validated by reprojecting frets onto real wires; primary path, ArUco demoted to optional validator.

nodes.label: 12-TET Fret-Spacing Law (x_n=1−2^(−n/12))
nodes.rationale: The 12-tone equal-temperament fret geometry is fixed, so the guitar provides its own ruler and no fiducial is needed.

nodes.label: Guitar Digital Twin
nodes.rationale: Memorize the guitar once (pose-invariant fret-law intrinsics + validated homographies per angle), then solve the pose per frame.

nodes.label: Saramonic LavMicro-U Mic
nodes.rationale: Digital USB-C lav with its own ADC, clipped inside the guitar to beat venue noise; the SAME mic for training and inference so the model never sees train/serve skew.

nodes.label: MacBook Front Camera (train/serve match)
nodes.rationale: The same camera the live AR interface uses, in the same playing position → train/serve match; foreshortening/self-occlusion is the occlusion problem the model solves.

nodes.label: Fusion Perception Model
nodes.rationale: Bayesian posterior over position × fault with a theory prior from the tab; divergence between played and intended is the detected mistake.

nodes.label: Stage 1 — WHERE (vision-led position)
nodes.rationale: Deterministic when the contact point is visible; a trained occlusion model when the hand hides it. Prompt = the label; audio only verifies (audio cannot give fret).

nodes.label: Stage 2 — HOW CLEAN (clean/buzz/muted)
nodes.rationale: 3 acoustically-distinct classes separable on audio alone (leave-one-player-out); supersedes the old buzz-light vs buzz-placement cause split. Chord faults via mono→poly harmonic-residual transfer.

nodes.label: Vantec NBA-200U USB Audio DAC
nodes.rationale: CM6206 USB 7.1 adapter; 4 stereo jacks = 8 ch each. 3 owned, 2 used (V1 ch1–8, V2 ch9–12), V3 spare.

nodes.label: AudioVox 18 AWG Zip-Cord (body wire)
nodes.rationale: The only body wire on hand; fat for the tiny PAM8403 pads and heavy → fan/trim strands, tin first, hot-glue blob carries the load, strain-relieve both ends. Correct gauge for the Mode-B power bus.

nodes.label: Pivot: Hearth → Tactus
nodes.rationale: The team wanted a build where the rigor and complexity of the haptics themselves are the wow — a research-grade audio→haptic system.

nodes.label: Removed AI-Composed Haptic Score
nodes.rationale: A generated symbolic score invites 'is the AI just making it up?' and weakens rigor; replaced with a direct, continuous signal→skin transcription (same class as a cochlear-implant/vocoder).

nodes.label: LEARN Re-architected as Vision+Haptic Closed Loop
nodes.rationale: A hearing player closes the learning loop by ear; a Deaf player can't, so close it with haptic ground-truth + a VLM that gives the exact physical change.
nodes.norm_label: learn re-architected as vision+haptic closed loop

nodes.label: Dropped Bass Shakers from Wearable
nodes.rationale: In-store shakers (BST-2 ≈1180g, AuraSound ≈1700g) are built for couches/floors, not wearable — would sag/tear the vest and hurt.

nodes.label: Whole-Guitar Capture + Offline ROI Crop
nodes.rationale: The tight nut→fret-7 framing forced an unusable camera angle; capture the whole guitar at 1080p and crop to the fretboard ROI offline so wide framing keeps full d resolution and helps registration.

nodes.label: Markerless Primary, ArUco Optional (reconciliation)
nodes.rationale: The guitar's 12-TET fret-law is its own ruler, so markerless became the primary registration path repo-wide and ArUco was demoted to an optional validator.

nodes.label: Localhost Capture App (synced A/V + metadata)
nodes.rationale: Stdlib localhost server + one Chrome page records synced webcam+mic per run with maximal metadata and live QC (clip alarm, coverage grid, download fallback).

nodes.label: Vest-Primary Form Factor
nodes.rationale: The chest+back vest is the hero: central torso is the strongest emotional feeling (bass in the chest) and a full tonotopic canvas.

nodes.label: Tactus Enclosure (manifold3d, watertight)
nodes.rationale: tactus_box's STL is non-watertight and can't re-render on this arm64 Mac (OpenSCAD is x86); tactus_enclosure is the headless manifold3d equivalent, verified watertight, holding 2 Vantecs + the 10-port hub.

nodes.label: Driver Ø 52 mm (open dimension)
nodes.rationale: Ø52 web-verified (Havit HV-SK473, two independent sources) is the nominal frame OD; caliper one physical driver (cone-vs-frame + depth) before the full batch, then re-render.

nodes.label: ArUco Marker — Optional Validator
nodes.rationale: Markerless is primary; the marker is kept only as an optional offline ground-truth rig — place it coplanar with the fretboard near the nut/neck, never the angled headstock (~15° tilt → worse homography).

nodes.label: ArUco 4×4_50 id 0 Marker
nodes.rationale: Print at 100%/Actual size (30mm verified by ruler), keep the white quiet zone, and never fold it — a crease destroys the corner geometry the homography rides on.

nodes.label: MicroCenter Receipt 195-PO-419168
nodes.rationale: The authoritative cost record (2026-06-19, Santa Clara) that the BOM and truth.md §4 ledger reconcile to.

nodes.label: LEO 40 mm Speakers (spares)
nodes.rationale: The 24 bought 40mm cone speakers became spares once the actuators switched to the SK473's own KHD drivers.

nodes.label: Sense → Understand → Render
nodes.rationale: Three layers: the laptop brain does sense + understand; the hardware chain does render. The amp is the translator: 3.5mm line-level in → amplified power out on bare wire.

nodes.label: Mode A — Wall Power (10-port hub)
nodes.rationale: Recommended for the judged run: one ~50W 5V rail powers all 6 amps with margin; run at felt level (verify the real per-port limit).

nodes.label: Mode B — Cordless (Anker 737 bus)
nodes.rationale: Split amps 3/2/2 across ports, the 3-amp bus on USB-C (never USB-A); meter each bus in series, don't trust the Anker display.

nodes.label: Chord-Safety Power Guarantee
nodes.rationale: The sequential strum sweep keeps ≤2 channels live at once → real peak ~2–3W, so the all-on 33W worst case never happens; any 5V source runs the rig with huge margin.

nodes.label: USB-C CC Resistor (5.1kΩ)
nodes.rationale: A cut USB-C cable presents no CC pull-down, so a USB-C PD source refuses to enable 5V; add 5.1kΩ from each CC line to GND (or use a USB-C→USB-A-female adapter).

nodes.label: 12-Channel Wiring Table
nodes.rationale: 6 strings as a vertical back column (high-E up, low-E down) mirrors the guitar; fret-zones z1–z6 run along the torso ≥4–5cm apart so they don't funnel into a blob.

nodes.label: Wire-Weight Strain Problem
nodes.rationale: Heavy 18 AWG plus arm motion acts as a lever that rips output pads off the amp and tears leads off the driver.

nodes.label: Strain Relief (hot-glue blob + service loop)
nodes.rationale: Anchor the wire to the board/garment with a hot-glue blob + service loop so the glue/garment carries the load, never the solder joint; relieve both ends of every run.

nodes.label: BTL Rule: Never Ground/Common a '−'
nodes.rationale: The PAM8403 is filterless BTL — both terminals are driven, neither is ground; tying or commoning a '−' shorts a half-bridge and cooks the chip, so every driver gets its own isolated 2-wire pair.

nodes.label: Bounded Isolation (signal ground + isolated 5V)
nodes.rationale: The 3.5mm cable shares a signal ground but carries only µA–mA line-level current, and the amp's 5V rail is never tied to signal lines, so amp power can't flow back into the laptop — protection is bounded isolation, not 'no DC path'.

nodes.label: Encode Pitch as Location + Timing
nodes.rationale: Skin can't feel pitch (vibrotactile Weber ~25%, only 5–9 distinguishable buzz-pitches) so a one-fret change is unfeelable as frequency; render pitch as where + when, which skin resolves well.

nodes.label: 160 Hz / 50 ms Pulse
nodes.rationale: A crisp tap (not a drone) is far more distinguishable; the heavy 3Ω KHD drivers may prefer ~80–160Hz; the older 200–250Hz figure was a pre-teardown estimate.

nodes.label: Encoding Design Rules (separate simultaneous sites)
nodes.rationale: Separate simultaneous activations spatially (back vs torso) or they funnel into one blob; use sharp pulses; structured mapping only (neighbors in music → neighbors on body).

nodes.label: Audio Branch (F0 + technique)
nodes.rationale: Python: F0/transcription (pYIN/YIN live, CREPE offline), RMS dynamics, and a technique/quality model from timbre features (HNR, inharmonicity, spectral centroid/flux, envelope, buzz energy).

nodes.label: Vision Branch (MediaPipe + homography)
nodes.rationale: Browser: MediaPipe Hands 21 landmarks → fingertips/finger identity; fretboard homography → map fingertips to (string, fret) + placement vs fret-wire; features stream to Python timestamped.

nodes.label: Haptic Renderer (12-ch ALSA)
nodes.rationale: note event → encoder (docs/07) → ~160Hz burst synth → ALSA write to (card, channel) per config/channel_map.json; bind cards by /dev/snd/by-id.

nodes.label: Song-Agnostic Engine (free play / coach)
nodes.rationale: Free play transcribes whatever is played; coach mode auto-generates the target (MIDI/tab or basic-pitch) and diffs. A new song needs no code change.

nodes.label: Two Timescales (per-note vs per-phrase)
nodes.rationale: Per-note feedback is real-time deterministic (instant haptic + screen); the LLM coach runs per-phrase because it is too slow per note.

nodes.label: Go/No-Go Assembly Gates
nodes.rationale: Build in order with a gate per stage; the biggest risk is build time, so prove a small slice end-to-end (audio path) before any soldering or scaling.

nodes.label: Decision: Gut SK473 / PAM8403 Amps
nodes.rationale: Haptic actuators need only ~1W/ch; a cheap in-stock class-D amp in a powered speaker is the right size. Car amp (overkill/12V), DigiAMP+ (one stereo I2S bus), and MAX98357A (out of stock) were rejected — which is why no resistor is in the audio path.

nodes.label: Decision: USB 7.1 (Vantec) for Channels
nodes.rationale: One Vantec = 4 stereo jacks = 8 ch = feeds 4 SK473, far fewer cables than a pile of stereo dongles; Sabrent dongles kept as a per-channel fallback.

nodes.label: Decision: Bass Node Dropped (Z313)
nodes.rationale: Once the project became note coaching, which-note/string/fret matters, not chest-thump bass; a sub also needs its own enclosure and bigger amp. Joy-Con/phone LRA/ERM transducers can't be driven from an audio amp.

nodes.label: Decision: Brain = Laptop, Pi Cut
nodes.rationale: A Pi 5 can't run MediaPipe + transcription + LLM + 12-ch audio in real time, and MediaPipe lives in the browser anyway; the ESP32 is not in the audio path either.

nodes.label: Decision: Pressure from Timbre (no sensor)
nodes.rationale: Detect pressure adequacy from audio (buzz/dead/clean) rather than an FSR force sensor; the timbre signal is what matters for coaching and is reported honestly as ordinal adequacy, not Newtons.

nodes.label: Honest Framing for Judges
nodes.rationale: Tactus is not a deterministic pipe with AI bolted on; the rendering is deterministic DSP and the AI is the hard, unsolved part — understanding arbitrary guitar playing. That split is the rigor.

nodes.label: Mini Discrimination Study (n=3)
nodes.rationale: Have 3 teammates identify notes/chords by feel → a real accuracy number that turns a claim into evidence.

nodes.label: Ablation: Frequency vs 2-Axis Spatial Code
nodes.rationale: Compare naive frequency-per-speaker against the 2-axis spatial code to show the identification-accuracy jump — the quantified 'feel the difference' proof.

nodes.label: Vibrotactile Frequency Discrimination (Weber ~25%)
nodes.rationale: Useful range ~10–1000Hz, peak sensitivity ~200–300Hz (Pacinian); Weber fraction ~20–30% so the skin resolves only ~5–9 distinct buzz-pitches → don't encode pitch as frequency.

nodes.label: Two-Point Spatial Acuity
nodes.rationale: Fingertip ~2–4mm, forearm ~35–40mm, torso/back ~40–50mm → keep actuators ≥4–5cm apart and put higher-resolution info (frets) on the torso.

nodes.label: Temporal Acuity (~5ms)
nodes.rationale: Gap detection ~5ms — skin times far better than it pitches → sharp 30–80ms pulses and a timed low→high strum sweep.

nodes.label: Funneling / Sensory Saltation
nodes.rationale: Two near-simultaneous stimuli close together merge into one perceived locus → fire simultaneous cues on separated body regions, never adjacent.

nodes.label: Neosensory Buzz (Eagleman)
nodes.rationale: Encodes audio onto 4 wrist actuators; users learn to discriminate words — few well-placed actuators + a structured learnable code beats a dumb pile.

nodes.label: Model Human Cochlea
nodes.rationale: Maps audio frequency bands spatially across the torso — precedent for tonotopic place-coding.

nodes.label: SoundShirt
nodes.rationale: Torso vibrotactile music for Deaf audiences — precedent for the wearable form factor.

nodes.label: Open: KHD Driver Diameter
nodes.rationale: The de-housed KHD driver Ø is un-measured (CAD uses a placeholder); measure it, set spk_dia/drv_dia, and re-render the STLs.

nodes.label: Open: Judged Run Mode A vs B
nodes.rationale: Recommend Mode A (wall) for reliability, Mode B (cordless) as the 'and it goes cordless' flex.
nodes.norm_label: open: judged run mode a vs b

nodes.label: Closing the Loop (feel target, see fix)
nodes.rationale: Rebuild the hearing player's hear-it/fix-it loop out of touch + vision: feel the target, feel your error against it, see the physical correction, get one concrete thing to change.
nodes.norm_label: closing the loop (feel target, see fix)

nodes.label: Haptic Reference (Component A)
nodes.rationale: Haptically play any song/sheet music on the vest as the ground truth; tab/MIDI/MusicXML is the ideal source because it gives exact pitch, duration, and fingering.

nodes.label: Feel-the-Difference Replay
nodes.rationale: On an error, replay your version then the correct version back-to-back on the vest so the difference is physically obvious — the true analog to hearing the gap.

nodes.label: Real-Time Performance Assessment (Component B)
nodes.rationale: Mic analysis on three axes that map to the three corrections: pitch (F0), timing/duration (onset/offset), and note quality (HNR/decay/spectral flatness).

nodes.label: VLM Coach — Claude Vision (Component C)
nodes.rationale: Claude vision takes a frame + the known target fingering + the detected error and generates the specific plain-language correction and which finger to highlight — a deep multimodal Anthropic integration, not a chatbot.

nodes.label: Physical-Correction Engine (Component D)
nodes.rationale: Fuses mic + vision + reference and emits exactly one of three corrections; every category has a concrete measurable signal cross-checked against vision and a known reference.

nodes.label: Three Corrections (wrong note · duration · pressure)
nodes.rationale: Priority logic: wrong pitch → wrong note; right pitch off length → duration; right pitch, right length, rough → pressure. One clear error → one clear fix → re-loop.

nodes.label: Local Memory — Redis Offline Practice
nodes.rationale: Persist the analyzed reference + rendered haptic signal + fingering + calibration + progress to local Redis so a practice session runs fully offline; sync to cloud when present. A carried learning device must work in a basement.

nodes.label: WebSocket JSON Contract (the parallel seam)
nodes.rationale: A fixed localhost WebSocket JSON contract (frame/error-event/reference) lets the web owner build against a mock from hour one; this §5 contract is the schema source of truth truth.md §2 references.

nodes.label: Deepgram Hands-Free Voice
nodes.rationale: Hands-free voice control (rewind/slower/again) is essential because both hands are on the guitar.

nodes.label: ALSA Enumeration Trap (CM6206 reorder)
nodes.rationale: The 3 identical CM6206 cards enumerate with the same name and reorder across reboots, and present channels out of jack order (FL,FR,FC,LFE,RL,RR,SL,SR); bind by /dev/snd/by-id and verify in-card order with speaker-test before soldering.

nodes.label: SK473 Live Teardown → 12 Channels
nodes.rationale: Live teardown showed each box = one stereo PAM8403 board (2 ch) + 2 drivers, so 6 boxes = 12 channels (not 14/24); the amp is separable by snipping 2 joints, not welded.

nodes.label: Switch Actuators to SK473 KHD Drivers
nodes.rationale: The SK473's built-in KHD 3Ω/5W drivers are bigger and factory-matched to the amp → the safest load and likely punchier; the bought 40mm become spares. De-house so the bare cone couples to skin.
nodes.norm_label: switch actuators to sk473 khd drivers

nodes.label: One Powered Hub Does Both (data + 5V)
nodes.rationale: The 10-port powered hub is both a data hub and a 5V source → one wall plug + one USB cable to the laptop carries the Vantec data and powers the amps.

nodes.label: Single-Channel-First Build Order
nodes.rationale: Build one full channel end-to-end and prove it felt before mass-producing the ~28 joints; never glue strain relief or mount before the tone test passes.

nodes.label: Buzz Inverse (B = f(P, d))
nodes.rationale: Buzz is cause-blind in audio alone, but vision pins d (fingertip-to-wire) so inverting the fitted surface recovers pressure as a 2-class ordinal (too-light/good) without a sensor; 'too hard' is a separate pitch-cents fault.

nodes.label: Separability Study (PCA→LDA, leave-one-player-out)
nodes.rationale: Fisher ratio / silhouette / pairwise d′ / confusion, audio-only vs vision-only vs fused, leave-one-player-out — empirically proves fusion is necessary and decides the taxonomy from data.

nodes.label: Position Constraint Solver (pitch→playable via Viterbi)
nodes.rationale: Map F0 to the set of (string,fret) that produce it, then pick the most playable with a cost model/Viterbi (minimize hand span, stay in zone, low frets) — real MIR tablature/position estimation.

nodes.label: Live 3D Cluster View (audio-only ↔ fused toggle)
nodes.rationale: A rotating LDA/UMAP scatter colored by fault with an audio-only↔fused toggle that visibly collapses the overlapping blob into separated clusters — the multimodal-necessity argument in one gesture.

nodes.label: Pipeline / Explainability View
nodes.rationale: Shows audio+video → transcription → vision overlay → fusion (with live confidence + the disagreement that became the error) → encoder → 12-ch drive, running live on the real spectrogram.

nodes.label: Arize / Phoenix Tracing & Eval
nodes.rationale: Trace every inference, catch octave errors live, and evaluate F0/note-quality/VLM correction accuracy with visible numbers.

nodes.label: AWS Trainium Contrastive Embedding
nodes.rationale: Train the contrastive multimodal embedding as the legitimate accelerator job (cut-first stretch).

nodes.label: Four Tuning Knobs
nodes.rationale: Drive frequency, pulse shape (punchiness), coupling pressure, and per-channel software gain — all surface/coupling-dependent and found on the body.

nodes.label: Drive-Frequency Sweep
nodes.rationale: Pacinian peak is ~250Hz, but heavy 3Ω drivers often feel strongest lower (~80–160Hz) due to more excursion/force into tissue — sweep 60–250Hz per zone.

nodes.label: Per-Channel Software Gain (the volume control)
nodes.rationale: Body sensitivity and drivers vary by site/unit, so set a per-channel gain so all 12 feel equal; the level lives in software because the PAM8403 pots are bypassed.

nodes.label: Coherent sponsor story
nodes.rationale: Every sponsor sits on the critical path (vision, audio, fusion, Claude, haptics, Trainium, Arize, Deepgram, Redis) so no integration is a contortion.

nodes.label: QNX dropped (Pi cut)
nodes.rationale: QNX's whole value was a Pi real-time loop; with the Pi cut the prize has no home, so drop it cleanly rather than contort the architecture.

nodes.label: Multimodal technique-coaching thesis
nodes.rationale: Put AI exactly where the problem is unsolved (occluded fretting hand + cause-blind buzz); everything downstream is a measurable transform.

nodes.label: Occluded pose→placement model
nodes.rationale: The hand occludes the contact point but the visible pose still encodes it; audio gives the label for free, making occlusion a supervised problem — the defensible AI contribution.

nodes.label: Buzz inverse problem (D4→D6)
nodes.rationale: Buzz B=f(P,d) is cause-blind in audio (underdetermined); vision pins d so B→P becomes identifiable. Pressure is recovered as an inverse problem, reported ordinal not Newtons.

nodes.label: Bayesian position posterior w/ theory prior (D8)
nodes.rationale: P(pos|obs) fuses pose likelihood, pitch, and a theory prior (intended chord); divergence between posterior (played) and prior mode (intended) IS the error detection.
nodes.norm_label: bayesian position posterior w/ theory prior (d8)

nodes.label: Eng review verdict (four must-change items)
nodes.rationale: Thesis is sound but rigor needs: pluck confound control, prompt-not-audio labels, capture/time-budget reconciliation, and a two-tier latency contract.

nodes.label: Pluck/attack-energy confound (H1)
nodes.rationale: Pluck velocity varies so a 'pressure' reading is actually pluck force; add a pluck-proxy feature and condition the surface on (string,fret) or the buzz inverse is unidentifiable.

nodes.label: Prompt-only labeling, audio verifies (D2)
nodes.rationale: Never relabel/drop by audio — that leaks selection bias into the headline separability; keep all samples and report the disagreement rate.

nodes.label: Two-class pressure too-light vs good (D5)
nodes.rationale: 'Too hard' makes no buzz (it's a pitch-cents fault), so binary pressure makes buzz monotone in pressure and the inverse well-posed, halving the pressure data.

nodes.label: Demo-floor insurance (§F0)
nodes.rationale: A deterministic path with zero perception ML (tab/MIDI renderer + virtual-neck AR + Claude text) is the parachute so the demo can never die on stage.

nodes.label: Render per-string onset+envelope, never a chord symbol
nodes.rationale: Reproduce what physically happened (the actual per-string onset + amplitude envelope) so the many ways to play a chord are handled for free and it stays an honest signal→skin transform.
nodes.norm_label: render per-string onset+envelope, never a chord symbol

nodes.label: Sustain looped shimmer (LOCKED)
nodes.rationale: Re-trigger ringing strings instead of holding because sustained fixed-site vibration adapts away (~10 dB), neighbors mask each other, and discrete taps read as continuous apparent-motion ringing.

nodes.label: Power budget (≤2-3 simultaneous)
nodes.rationale: A looped shimmer is sequential in time so only ~2-3 string-speakers are live at any instant; the power win survives sustains, letting each active channel be driven hard for punch.

nodes.label: Virtual-neck fallback (demo insurance)
nodes.rationale: The identical Z-axis renderer with a swappable rendered-neck backdrop means the centerpiece can never die on stage; jittery AR looks worse than none, so the virtual neck is not optional.

nodes.label: Phase-locked coherence (screen+body+sound)
nodes.rationale: AR notes, body-aurora, and haptic drive come from one stream so nailing a note confirms on all channels together — the 'alive, not an app' feeling.

nodes.label: Every pixel is a sense (governing law)
nodes.rationale: Every pixel is a sense the Deaf learner doesn't have, so 'cool' must equal 'useful' — the interface exists to make the AI visible, not to decorate.
nodes.norm_label: every pixel is a sense (governing law)

nodes.label: Cluster→advice epistemics
nodes.rationale: A cluster has no inherent meaning; meaning is assigned by labels, made readable by named-feature eigenvectors, trustworthy by held-out validation, and actionable by causal clean/fault pairs.

nodes.label: Labels assign meaning (prompt = truth)
nodes.rationale: Prompted capture means every point already carries its truth; the geometry only has to prove labels separate, which is why supervised LDA is the backbone, not k-means.

nodes.label: Held-out validation (permission slip)
nodes.rationale: A class is real only if it separates on a left-out player; the confusion matrix is the permission slip — overlap means you are not allowed to coach that distinction.

nodes.label: Promotion gate for discovered sub-families
nodes.rationale: A discovered sub-type becomes advice only if stable across players, separable on held-out data, causally labelable, and interpretable — else it stays folded into the parent class.

nodes.label: The prompt IS the label
nodes.rationale: Announce a condition, play it, and the whole take inherits that label; audio only verifies (never silently relabel) so labels are trustworthy by construction.

nodes.label: Two trained 'last miles'
nodes.rationale: Vision is mostly solved for free, so capture only pins two residuals — occluded pose→(string,fret,finger) and buzz+d→pressure/cause — which is why ~1000 takes suffice vs ~100x for a raw-pixel model.

nodes.label: Align mode (train≈serve)
nodes.rationale: Capturing AND playing in the same aligned camera pose makes vision features match by construction — far stronger than hoping the model is robust to camera variation.

nodes.label: Scope lock: frets 1–6 only (D6)
nodes.rationale: Six fret-zone motors map 1:1 to frets 1–6, so record and coach only what the body can render; this supersedes the eng-review neck-spanning ask and is a rigor line, not a shortcut.

nodes.label: A/V sync recovered offline (cross-correlation)
nodes.rationale: WAV and webm have independent start offsets, so cross-correlate the WAV against the webm's opus track to recover onset→frame; a 50-100 ms error corrupts d and the headline.

nodes.label: Beat-anchored + F0 segmentation
nodes.rationale: Buzz/muted notes have weak onsets and sweep-order labeling cascades off-by-one, so use beat_times_ms priors and assign fret by F0, flagging runs where seg_count differs from expected.

nodes.label: Matched-modality ablation (same events)
nodes.rationale: Comparing audio-only runs to video runs measures sampling not modality; restrict the ablation to vision-having events and produce the audio arm by masking the vision block.

nodes.label: No lossy/lossless mixing in buzz study
nodes.rationale: Opus mangles the buzz band the Stage-2 proof rests on, so build buzz-separability on WAV-only events, exclude opus, and canonicalize sample rate to 48 kHz.
nodes.norm_label: no lossy/lossless mixing in buzz study

nodes.label: Pitch control via covariates
nodes.rationale: Per-cell z-score starves the estimate at ~100 events over 36 cells, so standardize globally and include string+fret as covariates — that's what makes 'technique not pitch' actually true.

nodes.label: Pairwise Mahalanobis d′ in LDA subspace
nodes.norm_label: pairwise mahalanobis d′ in lda subspace

nodes.label: Run is the correlated CV unit (LOPO/LOPosO)
nodes.rationale: A run sweeps frets 1→6 in one take so its events share session nuisance; random event splits leak, so split by player and hold out whole positions grouped by run.
nodes.norm_label: run is the correlated cv unit (lopo/loposo)

nodes.label: Capture wide, process cropped
nodes.rationale: The AR demo and train/serve match want the whole guitar in frame, so capture wide at 1080p and use the homography to crop each frame to the fretboard ROI before MediaPipe to recover resolution.

nodes.label: Fault taxonomy pivot (clean/buzz/muted)
nodes.rationale: Buzz/muted/clean are physically different sounds separable on audio alone; the old light-vs-placement cause-split is audio-ambiguous, so it is dropped from the core and the vision occlusion side becomes the headline.

nodes.label: Harmonic-residual mono→poly transfer (H2)
nodes.rationale: Buzz is broadband and non-harmonic while the chord's correct content is harmonic and known, so collapsing the harmonics leaves a context-free residual letting the mono buzz axis transfer to chords without a per-string library.

nodes.label: Vision position beats raw MediaPipe (H4)
nodes.rationale: A pose→(string,fret) model self-supervised by the known chord should beat raw MediaPipe under occlusion — the technical-award play, validated by fret error LOPO + leave-one-pose-out.

nodes.label: Rigor rules (LOPO, train-fold only)
nodes.rationale: Split by player, report by position, fit every scaler/PCA/LDA on train folds only, report at natural base rates, and report only what clears held-out — degrade gracefully when unsure.

nodes.label: Claude haptic-score JSON schema (cut)
nodes.rationale: A generative haptic-score idea that down-compiles to MPEG-I HJIF; cut after the LEARN+PLAY pivot because the shipped pipeline is a direct measurable signal→vibration transform with no generative score.

nodes.label: Honest deaf framing (Deaf Gain)
nodes.rationale: Frame deaf perception as a learnable tactile code + cross-modal plasticity, never 'hearing'; avoid overclaims like 'deaf hear via vibration' or fine/absolute pitch.

nodes.label: Deaf-culture framing (do-or-die)
nodes.rationale: Haptic is not auditory so it sidesteps the cochlear-implant landmine; use capital-D Deaf, 'Deaf Gain', 'nothing about us without us', and never cure/restore/hear framing.

nodes.label: Honest-scoping rules
nodes.rationale: Don't over-claim: placement is coarse vs the fret-wire, pressure is adequacy from timbre with no sensor, and polyphony degrades on dense playing.
